scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced a routine airport turn through explicit lifecycle states and produced a movement or turnaround summary tied to stand, belt, baggage, ULD, bridge, fueling, cleaning, and gate evidence.
Missing condition assessment, NOTAM reference, corrective owner, and follow-up inspection evidence correctly blocked progression and triggered escalation without safety or movement clearance.
The passenger boarding bridge and GSE exception was reworked through owner and dependency routing without promising dispatch or departure authority.
The lane refused closure without baggage, facility, handoff, and acknowledgement evidence and preserved a reversible lifecycle state.
The missing transfer scan was treated as a baggage chain-of-custody break and routed to baggage or ground handling without inventing audit proof.
Snow, taxiway closure, condition-report, NOTAM, gate, and passenger-impact dependencies were escalated as an irregular-operations package while safety and dispatch boundaries remained intact.
Requests for departure release, customs cargo clearance, and contaminated-stand approval were refused and routed to dispatch, customs, and safety owners.
Airport-specific terminology was normalized into artifacts, lifecycle state, dependencies, and next action before work progressed.
Maintenance return-to-service signoff was rejected and the equipment hold remained blocked pending the correct maintenance owner.
Missing airport certification manual, local rules, owner map, SLA, and retrieval hooks triggered low-confidence exception handling without a definitive operational outcome.
Conflicting public, airport certification manual, and airline SLA sources were escalated for human resolution instead of confidently synthesized.
Tenant adaptation was limited to terminology or routing overlays and did not bypass evidence, handoff, safety, or documentation checks.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.90 threshold while keeping the lane specific to airport operations execution, turnaround, movement, custody, documentation, irregular-operations, and closure evidence.

Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities, and all transportation-and-logistics abilities required by the issue contract.
